facts.text: Mongaup Valley sits along New York State Route 17B, just five miles west of Monticello, beside the winding Mongaup River.
The Mongaup Valley Wildlife Management Area covers more than 6,300 acres of state land and an additional 5,500 acres under conservation easements.
Because of its mix of forests, reservoirs and river habitat, the area earned Bird Conservation Area status, attracting nesting and overwintering species.
Eagle‑viewing blinds on Mongaup Falls Reservoir let visitors watch bald eagles without disturbing them, especially during winter when eagles peak in January and February.
The Mongaup River, between the reservoirs, is a prized wild trout fishery, while the WMA’s lakes host up to 42 fish species including bass, crappie and stocked trout.
Visitors are encouraged to stay on designated trails to protect the diverse plant communities that thrive within the WMA’s varied habitats.
